blurb: A stiff dark-green rosette of upright, tapering leaves ridged with raised pearly tubercles. Tough, slow, and happy to sit on a bright shelf out of harsh sun.

care.light: Bright, indirect light is the sweet spot — an east window or filtered light. It takes gentle morning sun but scorches in harsh afternoon glare.
care.water: Soak-and-dry: a good drink, then wait until fully dry and the lower leaves give slightly. Less often in the low-light months.
care.soil: A fast-draining gritty mix, roughly half mineral to half potting soil.
care.temperature: Ordinary room temperatures; keep it above ~40°F and out of frost.
care.dormancy: An opportunistic grower that does most of its work in spring and autumn and rests in the hottest and coldest spells — ease off water then.
care.feeding: Very little — a dilute feed once or twice in the growing season.
care.potting: Slow and happy snug; repot every 2–3 years, mainly to divide the clump of offsets.
care.propagation: Easiest by twisting off rooted offsets from around the base.
care.bloom: A wiry stalk of small tubular off-white flowers well above the rosette; it doesn't die after flowering.
care.troubles: Overwatering rot is the only real danger; watch for mealybugs in the leaf axils.
care.toxicity: Non-toxic and safe around cats and dogs.
